Requirement: All UART and CAN connections must be properly configured such that the transmitting pin on one component is connected to the receiving pin on the other component, with pin assignments determined from component datasheets. If there are no UART or CAN connections, this requirement is satisfied.
Explainability: CAN connections between U200 (MCU) and U300 (transceiver) are incorrectly wired: the MCU's RX pin (PA11/FDCAN1_RX) is connected to the transceiver's TXD input instead of RXD output, and the MCU's TX pin (PA12/FDCAN1_TX) is connected to the transceiver's RXD output instead of TXD input.

✗ Net NetU200_70 connects U200 pin 70 (PA11/FDCAN1_RX) to U300 pin 1 (TXD input). This is incorrect - the MCU's CAN receive pin should connect to the transceiver's RXD output, not the TXD input. The MCU's CAN transmit pin (PA12/FDCAN1_TX) should connect to the transceiver's TXD input.

✗ Net NetU200_71 connects U200 pin 71 (FDCAN1TX - CAN transmit) to U300 pin 4 (RXD - CAN receive data output). This is incorrect: the microcontroller's CAN TX should connect to the transceiver's TXD input (pin 1), not to the RXD output (pin 4).
